Flow Matrix Rows / Procedures: inventory intake drafting in DirectIntakeWorkSurface; none found in matrix for pending-row edit rules or local row filter composition
Flow Guide Sections: Domain 16.2 PO Intake; Domain 3.2A Current Inventory Workbook
Discrepancies / Open Questions: The recording wants less tutorialized chrome, but current intake density and editability remain part of the real capability
User Job: Build a direct-intake session without losing row-level control
Capability: Draft and edit pending intake rows with supplier, product, quantity, COGS, notes, and filter context
Trigger: Open direct intake or focus a pending row
Success Criteria: Pending rows remain editable and operationally dense while submitted rows stop behaving like draft rows
Validations / Guards: Only pending rows remain editable; required fields stay obvious; default warehouse application cannot silently mutate submitted rows
Data Entities: inventory batches; supplier context; intake draft rows; notes; locations
Outputs / Side Effects: Pending intake work remains editable and reviewable before commit
Hidden Dependencies: Pending-state rules; default location selection; row filter state
Failure Mode if Lost: Intake throughput drops and operators lose safe draft control before stock is created
Evidence Required: Screenshot; row-edit proof; note proof; pending-state proof

Flow Matrix Rows / Procedures: none found in matrix for keyboard commit, selection sync, duplicate-row workflow, or save-state signaling in the intake sheet
Discrepancies / Open Questions: The pack review emphasized density and actionability, which only works if row selection and keyboard behavior stay intact
User Job: Move quickly through many intake rows without losing control of focus or selection
Capability: Preserve multi-row selection, keyboard actions, row creation and row removal behavior around the intake draft
Trigger: Select rows, press Enter or Escape, add a row, or remove selected rows
Success Criteria: Operators can move through draft rows quickly and top controls stay aligned to the focused row
Current UI Surface: DirectIntakeWorkSurface row selection, keyboard contract, and sheet header actions
Validations / Guards: Selection must stay synchronized with the focused row; at least one pending row must remain; keyboard actions cannot commit invalid data silently
Data Entities: intake draft rows; selection state; save state
Outputs / Side Effects: The sheet remains usable at operational speed instead of degrading into a mouse-only form
Hidden Dependencies: Keyboard contract; selection persistence; undo expectations
Failure Mode if Lost: The spreadsheet-native intake surface becomes slower and less trustworthy even if the API still works
Evidence Required: Keyboard proof; selection proof; add-row proof; remove-row proof

Flow Guide Sections: Domain 16.2 PO Intake; Domain 3.2 Inventory Movements
Discrepancies / Open Questions: The mock can simplify visual framing, but it cannot remove attachment lifecycle behavior or photo cleanup guarantees
User Job: Attach proof photos to intake rows without creating orphaned media
Capability: Preserve per-row media attachment, upload, and cleanup behavior
Trigger: Add photos to a row and then submit or fail submission
Success Criteria: Photo uploads stay tied to the correct row and failed submissions attempt cleanup of uploaded files
Permissions / Access Rules: inventory access; intake write; media write
Validations / Guards: Only images under size limits are allowed; upload failures surface clearly; cleanup attempts run after failed submit
Outputs / Side Effects: Photos remain tied to the intake row or are cleaned up on failure
Hidden Dependencies: Attachment lifecycle; row identity; cleanup warnings
Failure Mode if Lost: Stock intake becomes operationally messy and leaves inconsistent media state behind
Evidence Required: Media attach proof; upload proof; cleanup proof

Flow Guide Sections: Domain 16.2 PO Intake; Domain 3.2 Inventory Movements
Discrepancies / Open Questions: Single-row commit is easy to lose when the design discussion focuses on bulk speed
User Job: Submit one intake row safely with all required validations intact
Capability: Preserve single-row intake submission with validation and submitted-state transitions
Success Criteria: Required fields gate submission, metadata follows the right row, and successful rows move to submitted state
Validations / Guards: Row schema must pass; required fields cannot be skipped; submitted rows cannot stay editable
Outputs / Side Effects: A valid row creates inventory and leaves the UI in a coherent submitted state
Hidden Dependencies: Row schema validation; metadata notes mapping; submit-state transitions
Failure Mode if Lost: Operators believe stock was created safely when the row never actually committed correctly
Evidence Required: Submit proof; validation proof; submitted-state proof

Flow Matrix Rows / Procedures: inventory.intake; none found in matrix for selected-row bulk submit orchestration or intake CSV export helper
Flow Guide Sections: Domain 16.2 PO Intake; Domain 3.2 Inventory Movements
Discrepancies / Open Questions: The artboard pack does not make bulk submit and export obvious enough even though both matter operationally
User Job: Commit many intake rows and export the working session when needed
Capability: Preserve bulk submission, progress feedback, and CSV export for direct intake work
Trigger: Select multiple pending rows, submit them, or export the current sheet
Success Criteria: Bulk submission reports success and failure honestly, and export still reflects the current working dataset
Current UI Surface: DirectIntakeWorkSurface bulk submit and export controls
Validations / Guards: Only valid pending rows submit; progress and failure counts remain visible; export should include the fields the current surface exposes
Data Entities: inventory batches; intake rows; export rows
Outputs / Side Effects: Multiple rows can be committed safely and exported for downstream use
Hidden Dependencies: Selected-row orchestration; partial failure handling; export formatting
Failure Mode if Lost: High-volume intake becomes slow, opaque, or impossible to audit externally
Evidence Required: Bulk proof; progress proof; export proof

Flow Guide Sections: Domain 16.1 Purchase Orders; Domain 16.2 PO Intake
Discrepancies / Open Questions: The spreadsheet-native build must preserve the branch distinction between direct intake and PO-linked receiving even if the surfaces share visual patterns
User Job: Receive inventory through the correct branch and keep provenance intact
Capability: Preserve the distinction between direct intake and PO-linked receiving, including receipt-oriented downstream context
Trigger: Open receiving from a submitted PO or work in direct intake
Success Criteria: Operators can tell which branch they are in, and PO-linked receiving preserves quantity, receipt, and provenance context
Current UI Surface: DirectIntakeWorkSurface; PurchaseOrdersSlicePage receiving mode; poReceiving router
Validations / Guards: Direct intake must not masquerade as PO receiving; receive-eligible quantities must remain explicit; receipt and discrepancy adjacency must stay visible
Data Entities: purchase orders; receiving rows; inventory batches; receipts
Outputs / Side Effects: Inventory updates and PO state remain synchronized through the receiving branch
Hidden Dependencies: Receipt surfaces; discrepancy flows; purchasing handoff
Failure Mode if Lost: Teams receive inventory through the wrong workflow and lose traceability or receipt context
Sidecar / Exception Need: Receipt and discrepancy surfaces may remain adjacent
Parity Test Requirement: PO-receive branch regression with draft handoff
Evidence Required: Receiving proof; PO handoff proof; inventory update proof
